what_it_does: This file implements the Workshop AI generation layer: immutable diff application, Vi prompt builders for document/widget/wizard edits, candidate generation, template extraction/materialization, suggestion computation, a live document bridge, candidate/diff preview UI, and field-level regeneration utilities.
observations: The file defines a plain-object diff schema and routes those diffs through applyDiff for block, page, doc, widget, and wizard mutations.
Generation functions build prompt strings for window.claude.complete or cachedComplete and expect JSON-only candidate payloads.
Candidate UI previews are created by applying a diff to a cloned document and rendering a miniature block, page, widget, or wizard step view.
The bridge stores a mutable global active document reference plus save callback so other Workshop surfaces can apply diffs.
Template handling strips volatile IDs, asks Vi to identify variables, replaces placeholders, then re-stamps IDs on materialization.
Later symbols indicate additional support for cached completions, inverse diffs, streaming candidates, dismissed suggestions, rendered previews, workspace variables, and field toolbars.
standouts: Diffs are treated as the shared interface between AI generation, previews, chat edits, and application.
The file pairs generation logic with the React UI that presents and applies generated options.
It covers several substrate shapes in one layer: deck/brochure pages, widgets, wizards, templates, variables, and fields.
conventions: Uses global browser objects such as React, window.WS_BLOCKS, window.claude, and Workshop globals instead of local imports in the shown code.
Uses JSON prompts with strict response shapes and a loose parser fallback for model replies.
Uses plain object diffs with kind strings rather than classes or reducers.
Uses React function components and hook aliases with an _ai suffix.
Uses Date.now and Math.random for local IDs.
concerns: applyDiff is described as pure but generates IDs with Date.now and Math.random for some operations through ensureSection and insert cases.
clone uses JSON serialization, so non-JSON values would be dropped or transformed.
generateCandidates defines make but does not use it in the shown code.
materializeTemplate advertises kebab-case variables, but its leftover-placeholder regex only matches word characters.
Several generated diffs are accepted from model JSON with limited local validation of indexes, field names, or shapes.
The file mixes generation, diff application, preview rendering, bridge state, suggestions, templates, and field tooling in one module.
notes: This is the Workshop AI substrate ledger file: it turns Vi outputs into diff candidates, previews them, applies them, and exposes helper UI around that loop.
questions: Where are window.WS_BLOCKS, ViShape, window.claude, and rendered block components initialized?
What guarantees exist around model-returned JSON shape before diffs are applied?
Should applyDiff remain deterministic for undo, preview, or replay use cases?
How is cachedComplete configured and invalidated?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the shown behavior: the file centralizes Workshop generation, diff application, candidate display, suggestion handling, and chat/field edit support.
apparent_themes: Document substrate neutrality across deck, brochure, widget, and wizard forms
Global bridge state for cross-surface actions
intention_signals: The file appears intended to make Vi-generated edits usable as inspectable, reversible-looking candidates rather than immediate opaque mutations.
novelty: It combines prompt construction, diff algebra, preview rendering, suggestion surfacing, and field-level regeneration in a single Workshop-facing layer.
contribution: This file contributes a neutral AI edit substrate: typed diff objects, document mutation helpers, candidate generation prompts, preview/application UI, live document bridge state, and field/template utilities that a fused system could retain as the interaction layer between generated intent and editable design artifacts.
summary_for_embedding: AIEngine.jsx is a Workshop React/JavaScript module that converts Vi prompts into structured edit candidates, applies plain-object diffs to documents, and renders candidate, diff, preview, suggestion, template, and field-regeneration UI helpers.
intention_for_embedding: It was built to let Workshop users ask Vi for document, widget, wizard, theme, layout, template, and field edits while preserving a previewable diff-based workflow.

symbols.does: Deep-clones JSON-compatible values.
symbols.description: This helper returns nullish inputs unchanged and otherwise serializes through JSON.parse(JSON.stringify(...)). It supports the diff system by creating mutable copies before edits are applied.

symbols.does: Applies a Workshop diff object to a document-like object.
symbols.description: This function handles batch, block, page, doc, widget, and wizard diff kinds by cloning the input and mutating the clone. It is the local execution point for generated edits and preview diffs.

symbols.does: Normalizes a block section with an id, kind, defaults, data, and locked flag.
symbols.description: It looks up block defaults from window.WS_BLOCKS and merges them with supplied section data. It also stamps a generated section id when one is missing.

symbols.does: Creates a human-readable description of a diff.
symbols.description: It maps each known diff kind to a short phrase, including summaries for batch diffs. Candidate cards and diff UI use this to explain what an option will change.

symbols.does: Returns a display label for a block kind.
symbols.description: It reads window.WS_BLOCKS metadata and falls back to the kind string. This keeps generated descriptions tied to block registry labels.

symbols.does: Parses JSON from a model reply with a loose object extraction fallback.
symbols.description: It first attempts direct JSON.parse and then tries to parse the first brace-delimited object span. The generation layer uses it to tolerate replies that include extra text around JSON.

symbols.does: Returns registered block kinds except divider.
symbols.description: This constant function reads keys from window.WS_BLOCKS and filters out the divider kind. Prompt builders use it to expose insertable block kinds.

symbols.does: Builds a compact prompt description of available block data shapes.
symbols.description: It lists each available block kind, display label, and default data fields. The generation prompts include this text so Vi can return sections with expected field names.

symbols.does: Summarizes nearby pages and sections around a target page.
symbols.description: It emits one-line summaries for the previous, current, and next pages, marking the current page with an arrow. Prompt builders use it to give Vi local document context.

symbols.does: Formats one page into a compact context line.
symbols.description: This nested helper reads a page by index and joins its section kinds with truncated JSON data. It is scoped inside pageContextSummary.

symbols.does: Dispatches candidate generation by target kind.
symbols.description: It accepts a doc, target, count, and brief, then routes to target-specific Vi generation functions. It returns an empty array when no supported target is supplied.

symbols.does: Creates a candidate object with a generated id.
symbols.description: This local helper packages label, summary, and diff into the candidate schema. In the shown code it is defined inside generateCandidates but not used there.

symbols.does: Generates a short random id string.
symbols.description: It slices a base-36 Math.random value. Candidate generation uses it for local candidate identifiers.

symbols.does: Generates block insert candidates for a page position.
symbols.description: It prompts Vi with document title, nearby page context, insert position, optional user hint, and available block shapes. Parsed candidates become block.insert diffs with normalized sections.

symbols.does: Generates replacement alternatives for an existing block.
symbols.description: It prompts with the selected block data and other blocks on the same page, asking for same-shape alternatives. Returned data becomes block.replace diffs that preserve the section id.

symbols.does: Generates candidate pages for insertion into a document.
symbols.description: It gives Vi nearby page titles and available block shapes, then expects full page candidates with section arrays. Returned sections are filtered against the block registry and wrapped in page.insert diffs.

symbols.does: Generates whole-document transform candidates as batched block updates.
symbols.description: It flattens document blocks into prompt context and asks for alternative update sets satisfying a user instruction. Each candidate becomes a batch diff containing block.update operations.

symbols.does: Generates visual theme candidates for a document.
symbols.description: It prompts Vi with current theme, known theme keys, document title, and an optional hint. Each candidate becomes a doc.update diff setting the theme.

symbols.does: Generates full page layout candidates.
symbols.description: It asks Vi for ordered block lists tailored to the page and document context. Each candidate becomes a batch that clears the page sections and inserts generated sections in order.

symbols.does: Generates alternate widget data payloads.
symbols.description: It prompts with the widget kind, system, current data, and optional hint. Returned data patches become widget.data diffs.

symbols.does: Generates replacement KPI sets for a widget.
symbols.description: It derives the requested KPI count from target or current data and asks for sets with the existing KPI shape. Each returned set updates data.kpis through a widget.data diff.

symbols.does: Generates new wizard step candidates.
symbols.description: It summarizes existing wizard steps and asks Vi for single-step candidates at a target insertion point. Each candidate becomes a wizard.step.insert diff.

symbols.does: Generates alternatives for one wizard step.
symbols.description: It prompts with the current step and other step titles, asking for patch candidates. Returned patches become wizard.step.update diffs for the selected index.

symbols.does: Generates alternate values for a single field.
symbols.description: It builds an angle-specific prompt for shorter, formal, specific, different, or general alternatives. Returned values are packaged as field candidates with no diff attached.

symbols.does: Classifies a chat message as an edit request or question.
symbols.description: It prompts Vi with document metadata, the user message, and examples of edit versus question language. It returns edit only when parsed JSON explicitly says edit, otherwise question.

symbols.does: Generates a single edit proposal from a chat message.
symbols.description: It summarizes the active document according to type and asks Vi for JSON operations using the file's diff vocabulary. Parsed ops are wrapped in a batch diff with a summary.

symbols.does: Removes volatile IDs and timestamps from a document copy.
symbols.description: It clones the document, deletes top-level id and createdAt, and removes ids from pages, sections, and steps. Template extraction uses it to make substitution input stable.

symbols.does: Asks Vi to convert a document into a parameterized template.
symbols.description: It sends a stripped document JSON payload and instructions for identifying high-level variables. It expects variables plus a document with placeholders and throws if no parsable template doc is returned.

symbols.does: Substitutes template variables and re-stamps document IDs.
symbols.description: It stringifies the template doc, replaces variable placeholders with supplied or default values, parses the result, and assigns new ids and createdAt values. It handles pages, sections, and wizard steps.

symbols.does: Escapes a string for use inside a RegExp.
symbols.description: This helper protects variable keys before placeholder replacement. materializeTemplate uses it when constructing per-variable replacement expressions.

symbols.does: Computes local Vi suggestions from the active document and context.
symbols.description: It inspects document type, current page, section count, CTA/stat presence, widget KPIs, and wizard steps. It returns suggestion objects with labels, ids, and generation targets.

symbols.does: React hook for reading the bridge's active document.
symbols.description: It subscribes to BRIDGE updates and forces a state increment when the bridge changes. Components can use it to react to global active-document changes.

symbols.does: Renders the candidate selection overlay.
symbols.description: This component shows generated candidates, loading placeholders, keyboard navigation, close behavior, and hint-based regeneration controls. Picking a card calls onPick with the selected candidate.

symbols.does: Handles keyboard controls inside the candidate gallery.
symbols.description: This nested handler supports escape close, arrow focus movement, enter apply, and numeric selection. It ignores key events originating from inputs and textareas.

symbols.does: Renders a preview for one generated candidate.
symbols.description: It applies the candidate diff to a document and chooses a preview surface based on diff kind. It can render block, page, batch, widget, wizard step, or raw field previews.

symbols.does: Renders a compact preview for a block section.
symbols.description: It switches on known section kinds such as headline, hero, body, quote, stats, callout, bullets, metricRow, and statPills. Unknown kinds fall back to truncated JSON data.

symbols.does: Renders a compact page preview.
symbols.description: It shows the page title and up to four section labels. The component is designed for candidate cards rather than full document rendering.

symbols.does: Renders a compact widget preview.
symbols.description: It displays widget eyebrow, title, and up to three KPI rows from a data object. The widgetKind and system props are accepted but not used in the shown body.

symbols.does: Renders a compact wizard step preview.
symbols.description: It shows step kind, title, body, up to three fields, and up to three options. This supports wizard candidate visualization.

symbols.does: Renders a chat-driven edit proposal card.
symbols.description: It summarizes a proposal, lists visible diff lines with optional expansion, and provides discard and apply actions. It also computes a preview document by applying the proposal diff.

symbols.does: Renders one line item for a diff.
symbols.description: The visible file truncates at this component's start, so only its role can be read from its name, props, and use inside WSDiffCard. It receives the original doc and preview doc to display a per-diff before/after or description.

symbols.does: Renders a transform selection menu.
symbols.description: The body is not shown, but the signature indicates an open/close controlled UI for choosing document transforms. It receives the active doc and an onPickTransform callback.

symbols.does: Runs a cached model completion.
symbols.description: The body is not shown, but generation functions call cachedComplete for theme and field alternatives. It likely wraps prompt completion with a local cache boundary for repeated prompts.

symbols.does: Computes an inverse diff from a diff and document.
symbols.description: The body is not shown, but the signature indicates undo-oriented transformation using the current document as source state. It belongs to the same diff algebra as applyDiff.

symbols.does: Generates candidates with callback-based streaming hooks.
symbols.description: The signature accepts onCandidate, onDone, and onError callbacks along with the usual doc, target, count, and brief parameters. It provides an incremental generation path for candidate UI.

symbols.does: Generates one candidate for a target slot.
symbols.description: The signature includes seed and slotIndex, indicating per-slot variation within the streaming path. It likely delegates to the same target-specific generation concepts used by generateCandidates.

symbols.does: Reads dismissed suggestion ids.
symbols.description: The body is not shown, but the name indicates access to persisted or in-memory dismissed suggestion state. viSuggest and suggestion UI likely use it to suppress dismissed items.

symbols.does: Marks a suggestion id as dismissed.
symbols.description: The body is not shown, but it pairs with getDismissed and undismiss. It records that a suggestion should be hidden or ignored.

symbols.does: Removes a suggestion id from dismissed state.
symbols.description: The body is not shown, but it reverses dismiss for suggestion state. This allows a dismissed prompt or suggestion to become visible again.

symbols.does: Generates or composes Vi suggestions for the current document context.
symbols.description: The signature accepts doc, ctx, and count. It likely combines local suggestion heuristics, dismissed-state handling, and model-generated suggestions.

symbols.does: Renders a scaled block preview.
symbols.description: The signature accepts a section, scale, and maxHeight. WSCandidatePreview uses it to show candidate block outputs in the gallery.

symbols.does: Provides an empty callback.
symbols.description: This constant function performs no action. It is likely used as a default handler for preview rendering or safe callback invocation.

symbols.does: Renders a scaled page preview.
symbols.description: The signature accepts a page and scale value. Candidate previews use this when a generated diff inserts a full page.

symbols.does: React hook for Workshop document state.
symbols.description: The body is not shown, but the name indicates access to Workshop docs. Later variable and preview utilities likely use it to inspect available documents.

symbols.does: Substitutes workspace variables into text.
symbols.description: The signature accepts text and vars. It belongs to the later variable system indicated by useWSVars, countVarReferences, and auditWorkspaceVariables.

symbols.does: React hook for Workshop variable state.
symbols.description: The body is not shown, but the hook name indicates reading or subscribing to workspace variables. It likely supports variable substitution and field promotion.

symbols.does: Counts references to workspace variables.
symbols.description: The body is not shown, but the function name indicates scanning content for variable placeholders. It likely supports audits or UI badges around variable usage.

symbols.does: Audits workspace variable usage.
symbols.description: The async signature indicates a potentially model-assisted or document-wide variable audit. It likely reports missing, unused, or substitutable variables within the Workshop workspace.

symbols.does: Traverses a node tree with path tracking.
symbols.description: The signature accepts node and path, suggesting recursive scanning of document data. It likely supports variable reference counting or workspace audits.

symbols.does: Renders field-level AI tooling.
symbols.description: The body is not shown, but adjacent symbols indicate it measures position, fires regeneration actions, and promotes fields to variables. It appears to provide controls around the currently focused editable field.

symbols.does: Measures field toolbar placement or target geometry.
symbols.description: The body is not shown, but it is listed near WSFieldToolbar and likely computes DOM position for a floating toolbar. It supports keeping field controls aligned with the active editable element.

symbols.does: Triggers a field regeneration action for an angle.
symbols.description: The signature accepts angle and event, matching viFieldAlternate angle options. It likely prevents default UI behavior and launches regeneration for the current field.

symbols.does: Promotes the active field value into a variable.
symbols.description: The body is not shown, but the name and variable helpers indicate a field-to-workspace-variable action. It likely captures the current field and creates or binds a placeholder variable.

symbols.does: Binds field regeneration behavior to an element.
symbols.description: The signature accepts an element, getter, apply callback, and metadata. It likely attaches focus and blur handlers so WSFieldToolbar can regenerate or apply alternate values for that field.

symbols.does: Handles focus for a field regeneration binding.
symbols.description: The body is not shown, but it is listed after bindFieldRegen and likely records the active element or metadata. It supports showing field-level controls when a bound field receives focus.

symbols.does: Handles blur for a field regeneration binding.
symbols.description: The body is not shown, but it pairs with onFocus in the field regeneration binding. It likely clears or delays clearing toolbar state when the active field loses focus.
